metadata.title: Fix SAM3 Pre-Training OOM + MLflow 413 — Production Gate
metadata.critical-path: Task 1 + Task 2 (parallel) → Task 3 (Docker rebuild) → Tasks 4-7 (cloud validation)
metadata.estimated-cost: Code: 1-2 hrs. Cloud validation: ~$3-5 (4 single-condition jobs)

task.title: Add autocast() to ALL pre-training check functions
task.description:
ALL FOUR check functions in pre_training_checks.py call model(images) in FP32 without autocast. SAM3's ViT-32L encoder (648M params) OOMs on L4 at BS=1 in FP32. The training loop correctly uses autocast (trainer.py:417) but the diagnostics do not.
Functions to fix: - check_output_shape() line 52 - check_gradient_flow() line 83 (worst — has backward too) - check_loss_sanity() line 130 - check_nan_inf() line 171
All called from run_pre_training_checks() line 207-211.

tdd-approach.green:
1. Add `from torch.amp import autocast` to pre_training_checks.py
2. Add `*, mixed_precision: bool = True` to ALL 5 functions: - check_output_shape, check_gradient_flow, check_loss_sanity, check_nan_inf, run_pre_training_checks
3. In each function, wrap model(images) in: with autocast(device_type=images.device.type, enabled=mixed_precision): output = model(images)
4. For check_gradient_flow: UPCAST loss to FP32 before backward: loss = (output.float()**2).mean() # .float() prevents FP16 underflow REVIEWER CORRECTION: Without GradScaler, FP16 loss.backward() can produce zero gradients due to underflow, causing a false-negative ("no gradient flow" when model is fine). Upcasting to FP32 prevents this. This is simpler than adding GradScaler for a diagnostic check.
5. In train_flow.py: thread training_config.mixed_precision to run_pre_training_checks() call
tdd-approach.verify: uv run pytest tests/v2/unit/test_pre_training_checks.py -v make test-staging

task.title: Diagnose and fix MLflow HTTP 413 — investigate ACTUAL root cause
task.description:
REVIEWER CORRECTION (CRITICAL): The original hypothesis was wrong. google-cloud-storage IS ALREADY a transitive main dependency via dvc[s3,gs] in pyproject.toml line 46. It should already be in the Docker image.
The HTTP 413 root cause is NOT a missing package. It must be one of: 1. MLflow artifact store misconfigured (not using gs:// URI at runtime) 2. GCS credentials not available in the container at training time 3. MLflow client using HTTP upload path despite gs:// being configured 4. MLFLOW_DEFAULT_ARTIFACT_ROOT env var not reaching the training container
This task is now a DIAGNOSTIC task, not a simple dependency move: 1. Verify google-cloud-storage IS importable in Docker container 2. Verify MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI and MLFLOW_DEFAULT_ARTIFACT_ROOT env vars are set correctly inside the running container 3. Read sky jobs logs for the actual 413 error context — what code path is calling HTTP upload instead of GCS upload? 4. Check if the Pulumi change (commit 893c414) was ever deployed
See: .claude/metalearning/2026-03-25-mlflow-413-never-actually-fixed.md

tdd-approach.red:
STEP 1: INVESTIGATE before writing tests. - Run: docker run --rm minivess-base:latest python -c "from google.cloud.storage import Client; print('GCS importable')" If this PASSES → package is available, 413 is NOT caused by missing dep If this FAILS → the dvc[gs] extra isn't pulling it through somehow - Run: docker run --rm minivess-base:latest python -c "from mlflow.store.artifact.gcs_artifact_repo import GCSArtifactRepository; print('MLflow GCS OK')" - Read sky jobs logs for a SUCCEEDED DynUNet job (e.g., job 56) to find the actual 413 error traceback and determine which MLflow code path triggered the HTTP upload
STEP 2: Write tests based on findings (not before investigation).
class TestGCSDependency: def test_google_cloud_storage_importable(self): """Verify google-cloud-storage available (transitive via dvc[gs]).""" import google.cloud.storage assert hasattr(google.cloud.storage, "Client")
def test_mlflow_gcs_artifact_repo_importable(self): from mlflow.store.artifact.gcs_artifact_repo import GCSArtifactRepository assert GCSArtifactRepository is not None
Additional tests depend on investigation findings.
tdd-approach.green: Fix depends on root cause found in investigation: - If MLflow server config wrong → fix Pulumi + deploy - If env var not reaching container → fix SkyPilot YAML envs - If MLflow client code uses wrong artifact path → fix mlflow_helpers.py - If GCS credentials missing in container → fix auth setup
tdd-approach.verify: uv run pytest tests/v2/unit/deployment/test_gcs_dependency.py -v CRITICAL: After Docker rebuild, run a REAL job and check sky jobs logs for "logged artifact to gs://" — NOT just local tests.

task.title: Verify SAM3 HuggingFace model repo/filename is correct
task.description:
Self-reflection agent found HTTP 404 for facebook/sam3-hiera-large in job 68 logs. The HF repo or filename may be wrong. SAM3 is beyond training knowledge cutoff (Nov 2025) — must web-verify before any cloud launch.
CLAUDE.md Rule #10: "Verify Models Beyond Knowledge Cutoff."

tdd-approach.red: Web search: "facebook sam3 hiera large huggingface model" to verify repo name. Check if the repo is "facebook/sam3" or "facebookresearch/sam3" or another name. Read sam3_backbone.py to find the actual from_pretrained() call. Read train_factorial.yaml setup block to find the hf_hub_download command. Create test: test_sam3_hf_repo_name_valid — verify the repo string in model profile YAML matches the actual HuggingFace repo.
tdd-approach.green: Fix any wrong repo/filename references in: - sam3_backbone.py from_pretrained() call - train_factorial.yaml setup block HF download - Model profile YAMLs weights.hf_repo field

task.title: Rebuild Docker, push to GAR, verify fixes baked in
task.description: MANDATORY: ALL layers verified in same session per metalearning rule. "Code change committed" is NOT "fix deployed and verified."

steps.step: make test-staging — verify all tests pass locally
make build-base-gpu — rebuild Docker with fixes
docker run --rm minivess-base:latest python -c "from google.cloud.storage import Client; print('GCS OK')"
docker run --rm minivess-base:latest python -c "from minivess.diagnostics.pre_training_checks import check_gradient_flow; print('Checks OK')"
docker tag + docker push to GAR
uv run python scripts/preflight_gcp.py — all gates pass including freshness

task.note: Steps 5-6 require user authorization for GAR push.

task.title: SAM3 TopoLoRA → SUCCEEDED (end-to-end validation)
task.description:
Launch 1 SAM3 TopoLoRA condition. Check sky jobs logs for ACTUAL training output (epoch progress, loss values). "28 min job duration" is NOT evidence of training — must see training loop output in logs.
AFTER job reaches SUCCEEDED: verify MLflow has metrics AND artifacts (checkpoint uploaded via GCS, not HTTP 413).
task.success-criteria: sky jobs logs JOB_ID shows "Epoch 1/2" and "Epoch 2/2" with loss values. MLflow run has train/loss, val/loss metrics. MLflow artifact store has checkpoint file (>50 MB).

task.title: SAM3 Hybrid → SUCCEEDED (never tested, different architecture)
task.success-criteria: Same as Task 4 but for sam3_hybrid model family.

task.title: Zero-shot baselines → SUCCEEDED (sam3_vanilla + vesselfm)
task.success-criteria: Both zero-shot jobs reach SUCCEEDED. MLflow has evaluation metrics.

task.title: SWAG post-training artifact uploaded (no HTTP 413)
task.description: Run DynUNet with SWAG enabled. After SUCCEEDED, check sky jobs logs for "logged artifact" message. Check MLflow for post_training/ artifacts. If 413 still occurs → the google-cloud-storage fix didn't work in Docker.
task.success-criteria: sky jobs logs shows "logged artifact to gs://" (not HTTP upload). MLflow post_training/ directory has swag_model artifact.

final-gate.criterion: Docker image on GAR matches git HEAD (freshness gate)
At least 1 SAM3 TopoLoRA SUCCEEDED with training logs verified
